converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
George Lincoln Burr papers
Burr, George Lincoln, 1857-1938.
14-17-22
16.9 cubic feet. 16.9 cubic feet.
1861-1942. Letters, diaries, manuscripts. Letters, diary fragments, notes, manuscripts, and other material documenting Burr's boyhood in Newark Valley, New York, and his student days at Cortland Academy, at Cornell University, and at Leipzig University; his travels and activities in Europe collecting rare books and manuscripts for Cornell; his relationship with Andrew Dickson White; his relationships with other American and European scholars and his students; his work for the Venezuela-Guiana Boundary Commission; and his interest in the American Historical Association, the Cornell Alumni Association, the Cornell Christian Association, the Hall of Fame at New York University, the Telluride Association, and in many other social and professional groups. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 ======= 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2083975
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
B. Huth
Date completed:
February 1977
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, January 2003
Last modified:
Fredrika Loew, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of medieval history, librarian of the Andrew Dickson White Library at Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Letters, diary fragments, notes, manuscripts, and other material documenting Burr's boyhood in Newark Valley, New York, and his student days at Cortland Academy, at Cornell University, and at Leipzig University; his travels and activities in Europe collecting rare books and manuscripts for Cornell; his relationship with Andrew Dickson White; his relationships with other American and European scholars and his students; his work for the Venezuela-Guiana Boundary Commission; and his interest in the American Historical Association, the Cornell Alumni Association, the Cornell Christian Association, the Hall of Fame at New York University, the Telluride Association, and in many other social and professional groups.
Correspondents include Lyman Abbott, Charles Kendall Adams, Roland H. Bainton, Carl Becker, Henry Bourne, James Bryce, John Burroughs, Anna and Henry Comstock, Reverend Robert Collyer, Leonard K. Elmhirst, Livingston Farrand, Max Farrand, Simon Henry Gage, Daniel Coit Gilman, Louis Gottschalk, Evarts B. Greene, Charles Gross, George Willam Harris, Albert Bushnell Hart, Charles Haskins, Ernest Huffcut, Charles Henry Hull, Edward M. Hulme, J. Franklin Jameson (mainly having to do with the AMERICAN HISTORICAL REVIEW), David Starr Jordan (extensive, quite impersonal), Louis C. Karpinski, Horace Kephart, Waldo G. Leland, John Bassett Moore, John R. Mott, David Saville Muzzey, Wallace Notestein, Cuthbert Pound, Herbert Putnam (Library of Congress), Jacob Gould Schurman, George H. Sabine, Goldwin Smith, Preserved Smith, H. Morse Stephens, Dorothy and Willard Straight, Alfred and Ernest Sze, Ida Tarbell, Frederick Jackson Turner, Moses Coit Tyler, Hendrik Willem Van Loon (extensive and intimate), Oswald Garrison Villard, Booker T. Washington, Benjamin Ide Wheeler, Frederick White, Burt G. Wilder, and many others.
Letter, 1903, from Andrew Dickson White about his trip to Elba, with comments about Napoleon I, and fourteen postcards, some annotated, of scenes of Elba.
General
Cross references to added entries
Straight, Dorothy Whitney
Capitol fire of 1911 (Albany, New York)
Also,
Cassidy, Owen
Erasmus, Desiderius, 14667-1536
Huffcut, Ernest Wilson, 1860-1907
White, Andrew Dickson, 1832-1918
Morse Hall (Cornell University)
Russia (travel, 1907)
Travel (Europe)
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence
Series II. Personal Documents and Cornell Material
Series III. Photographs and drawings
General

Cite As:
George Lincoln Burr papers, #14-17-22. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
General
*Neither the Burr nor Schurman Papers contain other Gladden letters for this period (Sept. - Oct. 1893, but the Schurman letterbooks contain a copy of one letter from Shcurman to Gladden re preaching at Sage, dated October 26, 1893
Manuscripts for publication. Diaries. Elba (Italy) -- Pictorial works. Venezuela -- Boundaries -- Guyana. Newark Valley (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs. Guyana -- Boundaries -- Venezuela. Librarians. Historians. College teachers. Rare books. Rare book libraries. College students. Abbott, Lyman, 1835-1922. Bainton, Roland H. (Roland Herbert), 1894-1984. Bancroft, Frederic, 1860-1945. Becker, Carl L. (Carl Lotus), 1873-1945. Bourne, H. R. Fox (Henry Richard Fox), 1837-1909. Bryce, James Bryce, Viscount, 1838-1922. Burroughs, John, 1837-1921. Comstock, Anna Botsford, 1854-1930. Gilman, Daniel C. (Daniel Coit), 1831-1908. Greene, Evarts Boutell, 1870-1947. Hart, Albert Bushnell, 1854-1943. Haskins, Charles Homer, 1870-1937. Hull, Charles Henry, 1864-1936. Jameson, J. Franklin (John Franklin), 1859-1937. Jordan, David Starr, 1851-1931. Karpinski, Louis Charles, 1878-1956. Kephart, Horace, 1862-1931. Moore, John Bassett, 1860-1947. Mott, John Raliegh, 1865-1955. Notestein, Wallace, 1878-1969. Schurman, Jacob Gould, 1854-1942. Sabine, George Holland, 1880-1961. Smith, Goldwin, 1823-1910. Smith, Preserved, 1880-1941. Sze, Sao-Ke Alfred, 1877-1958. Tarbell, Ida M. (Ida Minerva), 1857-1944. Turner, Frederick Jackson, 1861-1932. Tyler, Moses Coit, 1835-1900. Van Loon, Hendrik Willem, 1882-1944. Villard, Oswald Garrison, 1872-1949. White, Fred M. (Fred Merrick), 1859- Napoleon, Emperor of the French, I, 1769-1821. Adams, Charles Kendall, 1835-1902. Comstock, John Henry, 1849-1931. Collyer, Robert, 1823-1912. Elmhirst, Dorothy Whitney Straight, 1887-1968. Elmhirst, L. K. (Leonard Knight), 1893-1974. Farrand, Livingston, 1867-1939. Farrand, Max, 1869-1945. Gage, Simon Henry, 1851-1944. Gottschalk, Louis Reichenthal, 1899-1975. Gross, Charles, 1857-1909. Harris, George William, 1849-1917. Huffcut, Ernest W. (Ernest Wilson), 1860-1907. Hulme, Edward Maslin, 1871-1951. Leland, Waldo Gifford, 1879-1966. Muzzey, David Saville, 1870-1965. Pound, Cuthbert Winfred, 1864-1935. Putnam, Herbert, 1861-1955. Stephens, H. Morse (Henry Morse), 1857-1919. Straight, Willard Dickerman, 1880-1918. Sze, Ernest. Washington, Booker T., 1856-1915. Wheeler, Benjamin Ide. Wilder, Burt G. (Burt Green), 1841-1925. American Historical Association Cornell University. Libraries Cornell University -- : Students. Universität Leipzig (1409-1953) Cornell Alumni Association Cornell University Christian Association Cortland Academy (Homer, N.Y.) New York University. Hall of Fame for Great Americans Telluride Association Venezuela-Guiana Boundary Commission

Burr correspondence and writing book1835-1902231Burr family genealogical papers, including sketches, biographies, family records pages from a Bible, and marriage records232-3Burr's pre-college notes, clippings, and memorabilia, mainly from Newark, Homer and CortlandMid-late 19th century234-6Burr's notebooks from lectures at Cornell1873-188024Class of 1881 Treasurer's Book, with notes and correspondence188124Printed lectures and exams241-2Printed Cornell material243
Scope and Contents
Annual Commencement Program 1879, Report of the Librarian, event tickets, Hill Jubilee program, Class Day Exercises 1878, Rules for Students 1878, Resolutions, The Library pamphlet 1883, Exercises for the Class of 1882 and 1879, and other printed pamphlets.Class of 1881 memorabilia1881191119221929244
Scope and Contents
Including Burr's oration "Revolt and Revolution"Burr's lecture notes and maps of North America245Letter book1884-188625Book memoranda25Small notebook25Book bibliography and memoranda, with small clippings from catalogues1885-188825Diary188525Transcribed copy of Balthasar Hubmaier's "Von Letzern und ihren Verbrennen"October190425Drawing book25Burr's description of books25Collegien-buch from Leipzig, with diploma folded in the back25Note cards of lectures and notes251-2Itinerary of Champlain-St. Lawrence excursion1883253Title slips for books used at Basel and in the British Museum1890s254Travel brochures255Cornell Club pamphlet, Cornell invitations, and other Cornell items1870-1890256Photocopies of book memoranda257Passports from the Department of State, with large watermarks188819041912258Class exams and notes, mainly from Germany1880s259-11Newspaper clippings2512Notes for Mrs. Hodder's Thesis2513Burr's handwritten copies of "Politics in College" and "The Religious Philosophy of Lucretius"2514Ithaca Congregational Church programs and pamphlets18841892189419152515Deposits in the White library, University library, and Christian Association library (incomplete)1880s-1890s2516Burr's application to military training camp191519172517Pedigree form for Burke's Landed Gentry19372518Young Men's Youth Association event programs and pamphlets1880-1884261

From New York, Cornell, and Washington DC. Also includes Burr's notes on membershipCornell University Christian Association invitations and pamphlet1916261
Scope and Contents
Includes notes from a lecture Burr gave to C.U.C.A.Note cards on a lecture called "The Living Gospel", possibly given at the Unitarian Church1920s262Notes, pamphlets, reports, and programs from the Unitarian Church262
Scope and Contents
Includes lecture "Talk on the Devil", "A Historical Development of the Christian Sunday", and "The Living GospelReports and copies of correspondence about the Red Cross263American Historical Association leaflets and pamphlets1884190919161932264Cornell Alumni Trustee nominations and biographies1880s-1930s265Nicola Sacco and Bartolomeo Vanzetti trial leaflets266Clippings and lecture on the Fred Morelli case1921267
Scope and Contents
Fred Morelli, class of 1924, took a leave of absence after being attacked by classmates for not wearing his freshman cap. Burr vehemently opposed the use of force and spoke out against the attacks.Biographies and sketches of Cornell faculty and alumni268Burr's war and Civic Federation lecture notes269Pamphlets, publications and leaflets of events at other universities1900s2610, 15Cornell resolutions, pamphlets, and miscellany1880s-1920s2611, 14
Scope and Contents
Includes invitations to events, program for "Alice and Wonderland", exam schedules, course listings, handwritten copy of "All Hal, Cornell!" by E.T. 1903, and petition to not take final exam.War relief materials1910-19202613
Scope and Contents
Includes war relief brochures, leaflets on Austria aid, documents from the American Relief Administration (including 3 letters from recipients of Christmas food packages), outline for a handbook on relief work for students in Europe, and Cornell Rifle Club announcementsAgenda note book189626Ramabai Circle of Cornell University treasurer's book with membership list and pamphlets1898-192026The Imprisonment of Ghent Professors, by Kr. Fred Morelli, class of 1924, took a leave of absence after being attacked by classmates for not wearing his freshman cap. Burr vehemently opposed the use of force and spoke out against the attacks.Biographies and sketches of Cornell faculty and alumni268Burr's war and Civic Federation lecture notes269Pamphlets, publications and leaflets of events at other universities1900s2610, 15Cornell resolutions, pamphlets, and miscellany1880s-1920s2611, 14
